target.name: Skeleton Technologies
target.founder: Oliver Ahlberg, Taavi Madiberk, Jaan Leis, Anti Perkson

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Skeleton Technologies is a Series E frontier-energy company operating in the Climate & Energy sector. The company has raised €392M to date. The comparable universe consists of 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, the peer set presents methodological constraints: only 0 same-stage comparables, 2 adjacent-stage comparables, and 0 similar-tier private companies exist within the narrow filters, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable peer-median triangulation.

Given these constraints, traditional peer-median valuation is inapplicable. The broader Climate & Energy sector contains 19 peers, but expanding to all-sector medians would introduce material noise from mismatched stage and technology risk profiles. The single public comparable (LNZA) provides limited insight without supporting private-market evidence at comparable scale and development stage.

A defensible valuation range for Skeleton Technologies cannot be established through the available triangulation methods given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peer density. Valuation work would require either (i) qualitative stage-and-risk adjustments from the broader peer set with explicit methodological disclosure, or (ii) asset-based or precedent-transaction approaches for superconductor technology and related IP. The primary uncertainty remains the absence of comparable Series E funding rounds and late-stage private valuations in superconductor and energy storage technologies.
